actions.name: Multiattack
actions.description: The tyrannosaurus makes one Bite attack and one Tail attack.

actions.name: Bite
actions.description: Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 10 ft. Hit: 33 (4d12 + 7) Piercing damage. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC 17). While Grappled, the target has the Restrained condition and can't be targeted by the tyrannosaurus's Tail.

actions.name: Tail
actions.description: Melee Attack Roll: +10, reach 15 ft. Hit: 25 (4d8 + 7) Bludgeoning damage. If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

tactical_info.combat_role: Apex Predator - Ultimate prehistoric boss with grapple control
tactical_info.positioning: Uses 10 ft bite reach for grappling, 15 ft tail for area control
tactical_info.damage_output: 58 total damage per multiattack (33 bite + 25 tail) with control effects
tactical_info.key_abilities: Massive bite with grapple/restrain, prone-inducing tail, 25 Strength
tactical_info.weaknesses: Cannot target grappled creature with tail, moderate AC for size
tactical_info.synergies: Devastating solo encounter, excellent for prehistoric campaigns
